gaps: No official Harris Teeter public OpenAPI spec was found for these logged-in grocery workflows.
The web app requires browser session cookies plus location/availability/fulfillment context for product and coupon endpoints.
Most existing grocery tools do not expose Harris Teeter cart, list, product, coupon, and store reads as a single read-only CLI.
patterns: Use Chrome session cookies for authentication.
Replay the web app's Atlas JSON endpoints with the same store and modality context.
Keep the shipped CLI read-only to avoid accidental cart or account mutations.

novel_features.name: Live Chrome session login
novel_features.description: Read Harris Teeter session cookies from the already logged-in Chrome session, falling back through agent-browser/browser-use when standalone cookie tools are missing.
novel_features.rationale: The web app has no stable public token flow for personal grocery data, so the CLI must reuse the same authenticated browser context the user already has.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this first when the CLI reports auth errors or a browser session has changed.

novel_features.name: Store-aware product search
novel_features.description: Search Harris Teeter products using the browser-observed Atlas search endpoint with location, fulfillment method, and LAF/modality headers.
novel_features.rationale: Harris Teeter product availability is store-specific and the Atlas endpoint rejects requests without the right fulfillment context.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to inspect current store-specific grocery results from a terminal or agent workflow.

novel_features.name: UPC product detail replay
novel_features.description: Fetch full product, offer, nutrition, inventory, and variant projections by UPC/GTIN through the logged-in Atlas product endpoint.
novel_features.rationale: The website's detail payload combines offer, inventory, and nutrition data that is awkward to inspect manually.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs exact item metadata without scraping a rendered product page.

novel_features.name: Digital coupon inspection
novel_features.description: List Harris Teeter digital coupons from the authenticated web endpoint, including optional UPC filtering.
novel_features.rationale: Coupon data is account/session sensitive and useful for shopping decisions, but the website is not agent-friendly.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to check available savings before building a grocery list.

novel_features.name: Read-only cart and list inspection
novel_features.description: Inspect the authenticated Harris Teeter cart and shopping-list surfaces without exposing mutating checkout or order actions.
novel_features.rationale: The CLI can safely summarize shopping state for agents while avoiding purchase or cart-write side effects.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to understand the current cart state without changing it.
